summary: Project concept chosen: SCAR — git-native negative-knowledge artifacts (deadend/fence/landmine) with edit-time agent injection
reasoning: Repo-resident published knowledge over per-agent memory; enforcement at moment of action over passive docs; agents both cause the problem and remove the authorship cost

summary: Advisory-by-default enforcement, max 3 scars/120 words injected per edit
reasoning: Blocking systems get routed around; alert fatigue identified as top adoption killer in stress test

summary: Validation-first roadmap: 6 kill-gate experiments before any product code
reasoning: Skeptic review verdict RECONSIDER — Copilot Memory and Lore protocol shifted competitive ground; core assumptions (agent scar quality, anchor survival) untested

summary: Kill gate 0.3 (fence-honor A/B) PASSED: 6/6 control violations vs 0/6 treatment, zero task degradation
reasoning: Pre-registered protocol; injected scars overrode even explicit user instructions while subjects still completed tasks within constraints; core behavioral thesis of SCAR validated at maximum margin
